data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: TSLA, DOCU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher Friday morning as investors looked ahead to the key U.S. jobs report, which could offer fresh insight into the health of the economy. Sentiment was also stirred by an escalating public feud between President Donald Trump and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) +6% – Shares looked to rebound after Thursday’s 14% plunge, which wiped $152B off its market cap, its largest single-day loss, dragging it below the $1T mark to $916B. The selloff followed a public feud between CEO Elon Musk and President Trump, who threatened to revoke federal contracts over disagreements tied to the spending bill.

Biggest stock losers

  • DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) -17% – Shares tumbled after the electronic signature company lowered its full-year billings guidance, overshadowing stronger-than-expected Q1 results. For Q2, DocuSign expects revenue between $777M and $781M (vs. $774.47M estimate), with subscription revenue between $760M and $764M and billings in the range of $757M to $767M. For FY2026, billings are now projected at $3.285B to $3.339B, slightly below the prior forecast of $3.3B to $3.354B. However, full-year revenue guidance was raised to $3.151B to $3.163B (vs. a $3.13B estimate). The board also expanded the share buyback program by $1B, raising the total to $1.4B.
  • lululemon athletica (NASDAQ:LULU) -22% – Shares plunged after the retailer posted weaker-than-expected comparable sales and trimmed its FY2025 profit outlook, excluding any tariff-related impacts despite heavy import exposure to Southeast Asia and China. For Q2, revenue is expected to be between $2.535B and $2.56B (7–8% Y/Y growth), just shy of the $2.57B consensus, with EPS projected at $2.85 to $2.90, much below the $3.34 consensus. FY2025 revenue guidance remains at $11.15B–$11.30B (5–7% growth) in line with the $11.24B consensus, while EPS is now forecast at $14.58–$14.78, down from $14.95–$15.15 prior and below the $15.03 estimate.
  • Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) -4% – Shares slipped despite delivering better-than-expected FQ2 results and upbeat guidance. Revenue rose 20% Y/Y to $15B, with semiconductor solutions at $8.41B and infrastructure software at $6.60B. For Q3, Broadcom guided revenue to ~$15.8B (vs. $15.75B est.), with adjusted EBITDA at ~66% of sales. CEO Hock Tan highlighted continued AI strength, forecasting AI chip revenue of $5.1B in Q3, marking 10 straight quarters of growth.

data.title: CyberArk Software prices $1.1B convertible senior notes
data.description:
• CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) priced $1.1B aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $750M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • Initial purchasers of the notes have a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $150 million aggregate principal amount of the notes.
  • The sale of the notes to the initial purchasers is expected to settle on June 10, 2025.
  • The net proceeds will be ~$1.07B (or $1.22B if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes).
  • Approximately $96.8M of the net proceeds will pay the cost of the capped call transactions, and the remaining net proceeds will be for working capital or other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price traded 1.1% lower on Thursday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Brown-Forman: GLP-1s, cannabis and Gen Z continue to weigh on demand
data.description:
Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.B) clocked its worst trading session on Thursday after the Jack Daniel's maker issued subdued guidance amid tariff uncertainty and weaker consumer spending.

CEO Lawson Whiting said weight-loss drugs, cannabis, and Gen Z drinking less alcohol continued to impact demand. GLP-1 drugs notably decrease alcohol intake and cravings.

"… it's the same big 3, the GLP-1s, cannabis and Gen Z, and we've been saying that for 1.5 years now," he said during an earnings call with analysts. "We'd be naive if we didn't say that there isn't some pressure coming from those."

"But I still would argue that it is the consumer and their wallet just doesn't have as much money in it," he said, adding that when people go to the grocery store, "in some cases, spirits has fallen out of the basket a little bit."

Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.B) shares sank nearly 18% on Thursday in their biggest single-day drop, after disappointing Q4 results and FY26 guidance. 

The company expects its operating environment to remain volatile, creating sustained levels of consumer uncertainty that will likely lead to another year of below historical total distilled spirits trends.

On the plus side, Whiting said spirits continue to take market share from beer and wine. "Premiumization is not at the same rate it was, but it's kind of stagnant a little bit, but I think that is good news for the most part."

data.title: European markets mostly lower as investors await global trade clues
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.22% to 8,830. The Halifax House Price Index in the UK increased 2.5% Y/Y in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.33% to 24,244. Germany's exports drop in April, denting trade surplus. Industrial production fell by 1.4% M/M in April.

France (CAC:IND) -0.19% to 7,775. France trade deficit widens more than expected in April. Industrial production in France unexpectedly declined by 1.4% M/M.

Euro Area inflation rate eases to 1.90% in May.

The Czech Republic’s trade surplus narrowed to CZK 23.2 billion in April.

Norway’s manufacturing production rose by 2.8% M/M in April.

Denmark’s manufacturing production rose by 2% M/M in April.

Retail sales in Slovakia fell by 0.4% Y/Y in April.

Retail sales in Hungary climbed by 5% Y/Y in April.

Industrial Production in Hungary fell by 5% Y/Y in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) almost flat, down 0.05% to 551.50, as investors weighed global trade sentiment. Traders adopted a cautious stance ahead of further developments in trade talks between the U.S. and China. 

In Europe, the ECB cut rates by 25 bps on Thursday and downgraded its inflation outlook for 2025 and 2026, reinforcing the case for a more accommodative stance.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area retails sales and GDP expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 5 basis points to 2.54%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.60%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Getlink SE reports 4% drop in LeShuttle freight truck traffic for May
data.description:
• Getlink SE (OTCPK:GRPTF) Friday said that LeShuttle Freight carried 97,125 trucks in May, down 4% compared to a year ago 
  • Since January 1, close to 500,000 trucks have crossed the Channel on board the Shuttles.
  • LeShuttle carried 195,294 passenger vehicles in May, down 1% compared to May 2024.
  • More than 770,000 passenger vehicles have been transported since January 1.

data.title: First Interstate BancSystem prices $123M subordinate notes
Fiserv to acquire remaining stake in AIB Merchant Services
data.description:
• Global payments and fintech company Fiserv (NYSE:FI) will acquire the remaining 49.9% stake in AIB Merchant Services (AIBMS) from AIB Group, making AIBMS a wholly owned subsidiary.
  • AIBMS, founded in 2007, is one of Ireland’s largest payment solution providers and a leading e-commerce acquirer in Europe.  The acquisition is aimed at driving continued growth for Fiserv (NYSE:FI) in Ireland and the broader European market
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q3.

data.title: France trade deficit widens more than expected in April
data.description:
• France’s trade deficit widened to €8 billion in April 2025 from an upwardly revised €6.3 billion in March, exceeding market expectations of a €6 billion gap.
  • This marks the largest trade shortfall since last September, as exports dropped 5.9% month-over-month to €49.3 billion.
  • Meanwhile, imports declined at a slower pace of 2.4% to €57.2 billion.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: HIVE Digital achieves 58% peak hash rate growth in one month
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) Friday reported a major operational milestone—surpassing 10 Exahash per second (EH/s) in global Bitcoin mining capacity, up from 6 EH/s at March quarter-end.
  • Additionally, HIVE's peak hashrate increased 58% month-over-month from 6.6 EH/s in April to 10.4 EH/s in May.
  • HIVE operates globally across 9 time zones and 5 languages, using 100% hydroelectric power to deliver sustainable computing at scale for both Bitcoin mining and AI data centers.
  • The company is on pace to reach 11.5 EH/s by the end of June and continues to accelerate toward its 25 EH/s target by Q4 2025, it said in a statement.

data.title: Amazon said to have cut jobs in books division, including Goodreads and Kindle units
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has reportedly laid off employees in its books division, including roles at its Goodreads review platform and Kindle units.
  • The Seattle-based retailer said the cuts affected fewer than 100 workers and were part of an effort to boost efficiency and streamline operations, as per media reports.

  • "As part of our ongoing work to make our teams and programs operate more efficiently, and to better align with our business roadmap, we’ve made the difficult decision to eliminate a small number of roles within the Books organization," an Amazon spokesperson said, as per Reuters.

  • Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has been implementing job cuts in stages, with recent reductions affecting its devices and services division, Wondery podcast unit, and teams in its retail stores and communications departments.
